title: == vs ===
subtitle: Similar, but different
goal: Understand the difference between == and === in JavaScript.
explanation: == checks equality after possible type conversion.
=== checks both value and type without automatic conversion.
Because of this, == can sometimes give unexpected results.
For example, 0 == false returns true because JavaScript converts types before comparing.
In most cases, it is safer to use ===.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: == is like a judge who says "they are close enough." === is like a strict judge who checks both appearance and document type.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think == and === are almost the same. In reality, the difference matters a lot because of automatic type conversion.

quiz.question: Which operator checks both value and type?
